lesson: Soft product and overtemp trips with high head pressure read like overcharge, but a seized condenser fan is the real cause. Check the fan before reaching for the gauges.
symptoms: Product too soft — won't freeze to serve consistency.
Compressor overtemp fault after extended use.
Machine serves acceptably when quiet, then goes soft during a rush.
The compressor eventually cuts out on temperature.
Cooling fades through the afternoon even though the room is cool.
No air can be felt leaving the rear grille.
An overtemperature warning appears after long runs.

tests.error-log: Repeated refrigeration warnings — temperatures drifting out of range under load. No hard fault codes.
tests.temp-probe: Condenser outlet very hot, head pressure high. The rear grille is silent — no airflow moving across the coil.

authenticityNote: A seized condenser fan kills high-side cooling, raising head pressure and degrading refrigeration. Pressures read high, which looks exactly like overcharge — check the fan first before reaching for the gauges.
